name: ceph
explanation: The Ceph check shells out to the local `ceph` CLI (`/usr/bin/ceph --cluster ceph status -fjson`, etc.) via `get_subprocess_output`. There is no network endpoint to probe: discovery means detecting whether the `ceph` binary is installed locally and whether the dd-agent user has permission to read the cluster admin keyring (often via `sudo`). This is a host-local presence check rather than a network probe, similar to Windows perf-counter integrations. Ceph also ships a Prometheus mgr module that the Datadog Agent could scrape generically, but that path uses the openmetrics check, not this one. Classification is custom because feasibility hinges on file-system presence and permission detection rather than any of the standard wire probes.
notes: If the user wants Prometheus-style metrics from the ceph-mgr Prometheus module (port 9283), that becomes a separate openmetrics-port-scan candidate handled by a different integration. The legacy CLI-based check has no canonical network surface.
